subject: UX & Research
title: The Checkout Flow From Hell
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Checkout Flow From Hell.

hook.text: A study tested 18 e-commerce checkout flows. The average had 23 form fields. Users only need 7 for a purchase.

buildup.text: Baymard found that billing address, phone number, and company fields are asked for but rarely needed.

discovery.text: Every extra field adds 1-2% to the abandonment rate. Cut fields from 23 to 12, and completion jumps 20%.

twist.text: Auto-fill, address lookup, and smart defaults can reduce perceived fields even further without losing data.

climax.text: Audit every form field: is this needed to complete the task? If not, remove it. Every field is a hurdle.

punchline.text: Every form field is a hurdle. Remove what isn't essential.

quiz.question: How many form fields does an average e-commerce checkout have versus what's actually needed?
options: 23 fields on average, but users only need about 7
7 fields on average, but 23 are needed for security
50 fields on average, with 30 being essential
